panels.title: Cluster Overview
panels.description: One row per target. Status: UP/DOWN. Role: auto-detected primary/replica. Slow/Waiting queries highlighted in yellow (>0) or red (>5). Connection % highlighted at 70% (yellow) and 90% (red).

panels.title: Top 10 Queries by Total Time
panels.description: Cumulative CPU time since pg_stat_statements reset. High total time = optimize this query or reduce call frequency. Pair with mean time and call count to decide: high total + low mean = too many calls; high total + high mean = slow query.

panels.title: Top 50 Queries by Total Time
panels.description: Full table of top statements. Sort by Total (s) to find biggest CPU consumers. The Query column shows the fingerprint — match it to your application code.

panels.title: Top 10 Queries by Mean Execution Time
panels.description: Average time per single execution. Queries here are individually slow — check EXPLAIN plans, missing indexes, or inefficient joins.

panels.title: Top 10 Queries by Call Count
panels.description: Most frequently called queries. Even if each call is fast, high frequency burns CPU. Consider caching, batching, or reducing poll frequency.

panels.title: Dead Tuples by Table
panels.description: Tables accumulating dead rows. High dead tuples = autovacuum can't keep up. Check autovacuum settings or run manual VACUUM on the worst tables.

panels.title: Dead Tuple Ratio
panels.description: Ratio of dead to total tuples. Above 20% = significant bloat affecting query performance. Priority: tables with both high ratio AND high absolute dead tuples.

panels.title: Time Since Last Vacuum
panels.description: Seconds since last manual VACUUM per table. Shows 'No data' if no manual VACUUM has ever been run — this is normal if autovacuum is handling everything (see Time Since Last Autovacuum). Only investigate if autovacuum is also lagging.

panels.title: Regressions Detected
panels.description: Queries whose mean execution time increased by more than the threshold (default 2x) between poll cycles. Non-zero = something got slower — check recent deployments or data growth.

panels.title: Mean Time Change Ratio (top 10)
panels.description: Ratio of current mean time to previous mean time. Values > 2.0 triggered a regression alert. Look for the highest ratios — those queries degraded the most.

panels.title: Call Count Delta (top 10)
panels.description: Change in call count between poll cycles. Spikes = sudden increase in query frequency. Useful for spotting runaway background jobs or N+1 queries.

panels.title: Connection Lifecycle
panels.description: Idle connections, accumulated idle time, and exhaustion prediction per target. STABLE = connections not growing.

panels.title: Idle Connections Over Time
panels.description: Connections sitting idle. If consistently high relative to max_connections, your connection pool is oversized or connections aren't being returned properly.

panels.title: Sequential Scan Ratio (top 10)
panels.description: Tables where most reads are sequential scans instead of index scans. 100% = every read is a full table scan. Add indexes on frequently filtered columns.

panels.title: Checkpoints: Timed vs Requested
panels.description: Timed = scheduled checkpoints (good). Requested = forced by WAL pressure (bad if frequent). If requested > 0 frequently, increase checkpoint_completion_target or max_wal_size.

panels.title: Top Tables by Sequential Scans
panels.description: Absolute count of sequential scans. High and rising = growing tables being scanned without indexes. Priority: tables with both high seq scans AND high seq scan ratio.

panels.title: Buffers Written at Checkpoint
panels.description: How much data is flushed to disk at each checkpoint. Steadily growing = more write activity. Spikes = bulk operations or missing autovacuum.
